member.name: Charlotte Cane
member.role: MP for Ely and East Cambridgeshire
member.synopsis: Charlotte Cane is the Liberal Democrat MP for Ely and East Cambridgeshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Neighbourhood Health Centres: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what population size Neighbourhood Health Centres should serve in rural areas.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Neighbourhood health centres (NHCs) will bring together general practice with a mix of community, local authority, adult social care, and civil society services, allowing staff to deliver more coordinated and effective care for better patient outcomes and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Education: Equality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the answer of 20 May 2026 to question 900062, when the Women in Tech taskforce will make its first recommendations on education and career pathways.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Women remain underrepresented across the tech sector, with the gap beginning in education, where fewer than 20% of A-level Computer Science entrants are girls. The Women in Tech Taskforce was established to address the barriers holding women back: to su...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Fuel Oil: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what consideration he has given to the introduction of a price cap for heating oil.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have requested that the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) undertake a detailed examination of the heating oil market and we will work closely with the CMA to understand their findings and develop options to increase consumer protection.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: Storage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, pursuant to the answer of 19 May 2026 to question 1352, if she will set out a timeline for including Battery Energy Storage Systems as an activity within the Environmental Permitting R
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra plans to consult Summer 2027 on detailed regulatory policy proposals for including Battery Energy Storage Systems as an activity within the Environmental Permitting Regulations, following further policy development, including through an Industry Lia...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Partnerships
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, pursuant to the answer of 20 May 2026 to question 1351, if she will set a timeline for updating the House on considerations of conversion rights between civil partnerships and marriages for opposite-sex couple
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government cannot provide any further information on a specific timeline, and as outlined in response to question 1351 on 20 May 2026, will provide an update in due course.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Women and Equalities

writtenQuestions.heading: Dentistry: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to the answer of 21 May 2026 to question 1353, if he will provide a timeline for the publication of Golden Hello scheme data.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answer I gave on 21 May 2026 to Question 1353, which remains the current position.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to support rural police forces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is introducing the most radical and comprehensive policing reforms in nearly 200 years. We will modernise policing in this country – equipping it to tackle more sophisticated, online, and cross-border crimes (like wildlife crime and organi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: General Medical Council and Medical Practitioners Tribunal Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of making regulations under Section 28 of the National Health Service Reform and Health Care Professions Act 2002 to provide regulatory oversight of the Gene
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made any recent such assessment. The Professional Standards Authority for Health and Social Care (PSA) oversees the ten statutory bodies that regulate healthcare professionals in the United Kingdom and social workers in England.It s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Overseas Trade: Israeli Settlements
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, if his Department will consider suspending all trade between the UK and Israeli settlements in the West Bank.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Israel's illegal settlements and decisions designed to further them are a flagrant violation of international law. We make businesses aware of the UK position to discourage trade with settlements, as outlined in the Overseas Business Risk Guidance.We also...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Uniforms
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what consideration her Department has given to the issuing of nationwide guidance on school uniform policy, to permit the removal of blazers, jumpers and ties when pupils are uncomfortably hot.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department has published non-statutory guidance on school uniform, which encourages schools to ensure their policies are reasonable, inclusive and practical. This includes taking account of the needs and comfort of pupils, for example by allowing flex...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services: Ely and East Cambridgeshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve access to NHS dentistry in the Ely and East Cambridgeshire constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The responsibility for commissioning primary care dentistry to meet the needs of the local population is delegated to the integrated care boards (ICBs) across England. For the Ely and East Cambridgeshire constituency, this is the Cambridgeshire and Peterb...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Dentistry: Ely and East Cambridgeshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the effectiveness of the dental recruitment incentive scheme on reducing dental practice vacancies in the Ely and East Cambridgeshire Constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The responsibility for commissioning primary care dentistry to meet the needs of the local population is delegated to the integrated care boards (ICBs) across England. For the Ely and East Cambridgeshire constituency, this is the Cambridgeshire and Peterb...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Shoplifting: Ely and East Cambridgeshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to help reduce incidents of shoplifting in the Ely and East Cambridgeshire constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Driving down retail crime remains a key priority as set out in the Plan for Change.The central aim of our police reforms is to protect and revitalise neighbourhood policing. We are lifting national responsibilities off local forces so they focus on tackli...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Education: Equality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what advice she has received from the Women in Tech Taskforce on equity in education.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Women are underrepresented across the tech sector, and this gap starts early, with girls making up fewer than 20% of A-level Computer Science entrants.The Women in Tech Taskforce was set up to change this: helping more women enter, stay, and lead in tech....
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Partnerships
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what consideration her Department has given to permitting the conversion of opposite-sex civil partnerships to marriages.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 2019, when civil partnerships were made available for opposite-sex couples in England and Wales, the Government ran a public consultation on the future of conversion rights between marriage and civil partnerships for both opposite-sex and same-sex coup...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Women and Equalities

writtenQuestions.heading: Batteries: Storage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what consideration her Department has given to including Battery Energy Storage Sites within the Environmental Permitting Regulations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to including Battery Energy Storage Solutions as an activity in the Environmental Permitting Regulations, subject to final policy review.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.answerText: While operators of Renewables Obligation accredited generators will have been aware of the expiry of their entitlement to Renewables Obligations Certificates for some time, Defra recognises the potential for adverse environmental consequences of the endin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Cemeteries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, when his Department plans to respond to the Law Commission's recommendations on the reclamation and reuse of graves.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Law Commission’s recommendations on burial were published on 18 March 2026 in its final report on the Burial and Cremation sub-project of a wider review of the legislative framework for burial, cremation and new funerary methods. Reports will also be ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the answer of 26 March 2026 to question 121006, whether her Department will consider underlying geology within its review of the funding formula for the distribution of capital funding to local high
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department expects to consider underlying geology, as one of the many conditions that affect the wear and tear of local roads, as part of its review of the funding formula it uses to distribution highways maintenance funding to local highway authoriti...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Transport
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she plans to account for the costs of transporting SEND students to school in rural areas when making future funding allocations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Central government funding for home-to-school travel is provided through the Local Government Finance Settlement administered by the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government. The Settlement uses relative needs formulae to assess each local au...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
